title: PR #391 blocked by code-scanning: pre-existing CodeQL high alert #131 re-attributed to the PR
context: PR #391 has all 4 required CI contexts green (Node 22 ubuntu/macos/windows + Node 24 ubuntu), is MERGEABLE, and is not behind main. Merge is BLOCKED by GitHub code-scanning protection over CodeQL alert #131 (js/resource-exhaustion-from-deep-object-traversal, HIGH) at src/validators/validator.ts:74 (ajv allErrors:true). The alert was created 2026-06-12 and is open on main; this PR does NOT modify validator.ts. CodeQL re-attributes it as 'new in this PR' only because #388 edits project-profile.schema.json, a data-flow input to the unchanged validator. No new vulnerability is introduced by this PR.

options.label: Dismiss alert #131 as pre-existing (won't-fix/accepted) to unblock, note in PR
Fix validator.ts (ajv input guard) in this PR — scope creep beyond the 5 issues
Leave PR blocked; open a separate issue/PR to fix #131 on main first
Admin-override merge past the code-scanning gate

rationale: All 4 required CI contexts pass; the blocker is a pre-existing CodeQL high alert (#131) re-attributed via the #388 schema change. Owner chose to fix the ajv allErrors DoS sink in validator.ts within this PR rather than dismiss the alert.
